name: weatherbench2

data_origin.blend: ERA5 reanalysis = observations + assimilation model (data + sim blend)

env_axis.name: year
env_axis.shift_mechanism: canonical temporal split (≤2018 train / 2019 val / 2020 test); cross-source axis (ERA5 vs IFS-analysis) is a separate pair

facts: ERA5 reanalysis 1959-2023, 6-hourly, 0.25°; canonical splits (≤2018 / 2019 / 2020); GraphCast/Pangu/FourCastNet/IFS baselines.
hypotheses: The temporal split is a natural shift driven by climate-change baseline drift rather than a mere convenience split.
ML weather models learn ERA5-specific artifacts rather than generalizing from ERA5 training to IFS-analyses test, so cross-source transfer within climate science degrades.
intuitions: A WeatherBench 2 leaderboard with 2024-25 results (GenCast, Aurora) likely exists.
ECMWF AIFS operational-deployment papers have probably been published.
Cross-reanalysis comparisons with MERRA-2 and JRA-3Q likely exist.
unknowns: ERA5 blends observation and model via assimilation; whether OOD on ERA5 measures physical generalization or reanalysis-product generalization cannot be determined from the dataset alone.
The 0.25°/6-hour grain is a fixed commitment; whether atmospheric dynamics are captured at this resolution cannot be determined from ERA5 itself.
epistemic_state.boundary: An array-regime dataset (Zarr-native, no traces.parquet) of mixed observation/model origin; the cross-source axis (ERA5 vs IFS) is a separate pair from the temporal split.

representational_commitments: Materialized subset: 64x32 grid (5.625deg) at 6-hourly; full 0.25deg variant available on gs://weatherbench2/ but ~TB-scale, deferred for our snapshot
Multi-variable bundling (T, u, v, q, ...) at fixed grain
Zarr store is multi-file chunked; no single-file SHA256 — provenance via gs://weatherbench2/ public bucket + snapshot_date
